name: Mean Reversion
description: Counter-move model that fades statistically stretched price once rejection and re-acceptance back toward value appear.

context: Session mean, VWAP, and prior swing extremes identified.
Price extended into a known liquidity pocket or deviation zone.
First clean rejection after the sweep, not a repeated fade attempt.
setup: Outer band, prior high or low, or deviation threshold tagged.
Rejection wick or failed auction printed at the extreme.
Momentum slowed and closes stopped progressing in trend direction.
trigger: Reclaim back inside the prior range after the sweep.
Higher-low or lower-high break confirming the turn.
Add-on only if the rejection structure tightens invalidation.
confirmation: Stretch or displacement visibly extended relative to recent auction.
Extreme rejected with wick imbalance or failed breakout behavior.
Path back to mean open without an immediate chop barrier.
invalidation: Acceptance outside the deviation zone with consecutive closes.
New session expansion resetting the regime.
noTrade: High-impact news resetting the market regime.
Mean too close to offer a clean reversion path.
Strong multi-timeframe momentum still active without exhaustion.

stopLoss: Protective stop beyond sweep high or low, or beyond the deviation band.
Stop location outside expected noise and beyond the rejection midpoint.
Invalidation distance remaining inside planned tolerance.
takeProfit: First target at local mean, VWAP, or midrange.
Second target at the opposite side of balance if reversal strength expands.
Partials secured once price reclaims the mean.
timeExit: Reversion starting within 2 to 5 candles after entry.
Session-specific fade closed before the next active reopen.
Position closed when range conditions disappear.
earlyExit: Extreme reclaimed and held after entry.
Mean touched without continuation through balance.
Fresh impulse candle negating the rejection structure.
